internal_issues.description: SEC-5 Test 6 verdict paragraph cited fabricated '30% falsification threshold'. Dome's actual threshold is >50% for 3 consecutive years. 39.9% does NOT exceed 50% — text was factually wrong AND self-contradictory with TLDR/body.

suggested_patch.find: (b) exceeds the dome's own 30% falsification threshold,
suggested_patch.replace: (b) approaches the dome's own >50% falsification threshold (which requires >50% error for 3 consecutive years — a criterion so generous that nearly-half-wrong predictions still pass),

internal_issues.description: SEC-5 Test 6 body had duplicate opening sentence from EXP-082 merge artifact. 'Shows failure' contradicted the dome-says-PASS table.

suggested_patch.find: The dome's own tracking shows failure. The dome's own tracking shows substantial error.
suggested_patch.replace: The dome's own tracking shows substantial error.

internal_issues.description: SEC-5 opening paragraph said NMP 'self-reports as failing' when dome says PASS. Fixed to 'self-reports as passing at 39.9% error — though only by widening the tolerance'.
WIN-065 cited wrong n(r) formula (quadratic vs dome's exponential). Fixed formula citation; refraction estimate (~0.04°) remains approximately correct.
SEC-1.8 overstated AE projection correspondence with 'exactly as'. Softened to 'analogously to how' — correspondence is approximate (ratio 0.96-1.94), not exact.
SEC-1.8 '920× stronger medium → 15° shift' uses linear scaling that may not hold for bulk media. Needs parenthetical caveat. Assigned to EXP-096.
SEC-1.8 missing defense: n(r) empirically calibrated to Polaris elevations — circularity response needed. Assigned to EXP-096.
SEC-4.1 Polaris contradiction (ISS-779). Analyst EXP-095 verified all 7 holes already addressed in current text. No changes needed.

recommended_actions.action: EXP-096 (SEC-1.8 prose additions): analyst should add refraction linear-scaling caveat (ISS-865) and dome defense for n(r) calibration circularity (ISS-866). High priority — SEC-1.8 is the strongest section and deserves defense-hardening.
